name: Nori Maki
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the rice and kombu, then cook according to package instructions.
2. Dissolve sugar in a mixture of rice vinegar and water until completely dissolved.
3. Mix in sea salt to taste (adjust as desired).
4. Combine cooked rice with the dressing and stir gently until well mixed.
5. Spread the rice onto a large bowl and let cool slightly.
6. Arrange 8 sheets of nori on a sushi mat, aligning it along its long axis.
7. Cover about two-thirds of the nori sheet with a thin layer of rice (about 1 cm thick), leaving half an inch bare at the bottom and top edges.
8. Place desired fillings in a line across the middle of the rice.
9. Fold the nori over the filling, tucking in the short edge into itself.
10. Wrap the sushi mat around the roll to pack the rice firmly without cracking the nori.
11. Use gentle pressure to shape the roll into a round form that can be easily sliced with a sharp knife.
12. Allow the rolls to cool before slicing and serving. Leftover maki makes an excellent lunch.
